channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: Three journalists killed in Israeli strike on marked press car in Lebanon
item.description: Lebanon's president condemns 'blatant crime', as Israeli strikes kill more journalists.

item.title: One month in, disapproval high but US lawmakers take no action on Iran war
item.description: Republicans and Democrats remain entrenched despite public polling showing dismay over war, petrol prices.

item.title: Iran warns neighbours not to let ‘enemies run the war’ from their land
item.description: President Pezeshkian's warning is the latest by Tehran to neighbours not to allow their territory to be used in the war.

item.title: At least 15 US troops wounded in Iran strike on Saudi airbase: Reports
item.description: At least five soldiers are reported in serious condition following Iran attack that damaged refuelling aircraft.

item.title: Iran footballers pay tribute to victims of school strike at start of war
item.description: The Iran national team played for the first time since the Middle East conflict began, losing 2-1 to Nigeria.

item.title: Former US defence chief says more war won’t bring peace to Middle East
item.description: Leon Panetta, former US Secretary of Defence, says that there is no clear end to the war in Iran.

item.title: Protests in Yemen against US-Israeli attacks as Houthis warn of action
item.description: Protesters in Yemen took to the streets to voice support for Iran, Palestine, and Lebanon amid US and Israeli attacks.

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill 4 as deal to end war dims
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio voices openness to diverting weapons to Kyiv to support the US attack on Iran.

item.title: The war on Iran faces a MAGA backlash
item.description: A deep split in the MAGA movement threatens Trump’s Iran war.

item.title: Philippine transport strikers say Marcos Jr failing to control oil prices
item.description: Transport workers call on Philippines President Marcos Jr to focus on price caps and reining in oil companies.
